body.title: Librarian: overnight inference learning pipeline
body.result: learning_pipeline.py + tests: queue consumption → epistemic knowledge nodes in adc.palace; 3+ query class examples → node creation; logged with stats
body.description:
Librarian overnight job that consumes the learning queue populated by T-librarian-inference-proxy. For each (request, response, log_pointer) tuple, builds or extends graph-tree nodes that could answer that query class without hitting an LLM next time.

Key design constraint: this knowledge graph is PURELY EPISTEMIC — no emotional encoding. Igor's graph trees carry emotional salience (CAPS = distress, that's load-bearing for his cognition). This graph is an observation layer: 'Akien was upset' may be noted as fact but is NOT encoded to produce an emotional reaction in any agent. The distinction is architectural, not just philosophical.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/librarian/learning_pipeline.py (new); librarian knowledge graph storage (TBD — likely adc.palace or new table)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; purely epistemic encoding (no emotional salience in this graph); log pipeline runs at INFO

**Scope boundary:** IN — consume learning queue; build/extend knowledge graph nodes; epistemic-only encoding. OUT — chat log retroactive bootstrap (T-chat-log-learning-bootstrap); emotional encoding rules (those stay in Igor)

**Completion criteria:** (1) pipeline consumes queue and produces graph nodes; (2) a query class seen 3+ times produces a node that the proxy pre-check can match; (3) no emotional salience fields on knowledge graph nodes (schema check); (4) pipeline run logged with node counts
